query: Meta Muse Spark 1.1 AI model unveiling performance claims

mako.raw:
{"query":"Meta Muse Spark 1.1 AI model unveiling performance claims","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1552,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://ai.meta.com/blog/introducing-muse-spark-meta-model-api/","title":"Introducing Muse Spark 1.1","content":"Muse Spark 1.1 is a multimodal reasoning model built for agentic tasks, with major gains in tool and computer use, coding, and multimodal understanding.
Muse Spark 1.1 delivers exceptional performance in personal agentic tasks that require planning and orchestration across a range of external apps and services.
Agentic dinner party organization: In real-world applications, new context arises that changes the task.
Coding performance for Muse Spark 1.1 improved substantially on real-world tasks involving large, complex codebases.
It can diagnose and fix complex bugs, implement new features in enterprise-grade systems, and execute large code migrations.
It can interact with real environments and produce grounded outputs with strengths in visual-to-code artifact generation, ultra-descriptive image and video captioning, and agentic workflow execution for multimodal use cases."},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/meta-enters-the-crowded-ai-coding-battle-with-muse-spark-1-1/","title":"Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1 | TechCrunch","content":"Meta publicly launched a new version of Muse Spark on Thursday, a multimodal AI model designed for agentic coding that aims to compete with similar products offered by OpenAI and Anthropic.
Spark 1.1, the first version of which was announced in April, can engage in multistep reasoning and handle complex processes, manage digital workflows, and deploy new features in enterprise systems, the company says.
In his post, Zuckerberg called Spark “a strong agentic and coding model at a very low price,” noting that the model was “strongest at agentic performance, tool use, and computer use.”
Zuckerberg also noted that there was “more to come soon” — implying that the company plans to release additional models."},{"url":"https://fortune.com/2026/07/09/meta-muse-spark-1-1-release-alexandr-wang-superintelligence-labs-mark-zuckerberg/","title":"Meta releases latest update of AI model Muse Spark | Fortune","content":"Add Fortune on Google for similar content.
Meta released a new version of Muse Spark on Thursday, claiming that the AI model surpasses the capabilities of prior products from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
The tech giant did not say how its new AI product compares to OpenAI and Anthropic’s most recent model releases.
Originally committed to building open-source models, the tech giant pivoted after it spent $14.3 billion in 2025 to acquire a 49% non-voting stake in Scale AI, Wang’s AI data startup that skyrocketed in prominence amid the AI boom.
Wang reorganized the company’s AI efforts into what it now calls Meta Superintelligence Labs.
Staffers felt whiplashed as Wang and Zuckerberg rebuilt the company’s AI team from the ground up.
On Tuesday, Meta also released Muse Image and Muse Video, the first models released from Superintelligence Labs focused on image and video generation.
Every Tuesday, this new newsletter delivers clear-eyed, authoritative intelligence on the deals, decisions, policies, and power shifts shaping one of the world’s most consequential regions, written for the people who need to act on it."}]}

{"query":"Meta Muse Spark 1.1 AI model unveiling performance claims","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://fortune.com/2026/07/09/meta-muse-spark-1-1-release-alexandr-wang-superintelligence-labs-mark-zuckerberg","title":"Meta releases latest update of AI model Muse Spark","content":"# Meta releases latest update of AI model Muse Spark as tech giant accelerates AI push under Alexandr Wang. Alexandr Wang,  chief AI officer of Meta. Meta released a new version of Muse Spark on Thursday, claiming that the AI model surpasses the capabilities of prior products from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. Dubbed Muse Spark 1.1, the new model is proficient across a range of tasks, including coding, video captioning, and reasoning, Meta said in a blog post. The update to Muse Spark beats out Google’s latest release of Gemini in benchmarks measuring coding and reasoning capabilities, according to the tech giant. And Meta’s new AI entry surpasses older versions of OpenAI and Anthropic’s models on some verticals, the company said. The release of Muse Spark 1.1 comes just three months after Meta unveiled its first significant entry into the AI race under a reorganized unit headed by Alexandr Wang, Meta’s first-ever chief AI officer.","score":0.8969848,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/meta-enters-the-crowded-ai-coding-battle-with-muse-spark-1-1","title":"Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark ...","content":"# Meta enters the crowded AI coding battle with Muse Spark 1.1. Meta publicly launched a new version of Muse Spark on Thursday, a multimodal AI model designed for agentic coding that aims to compete with similar products offered by OpenAI and Anthropic. Spark 1.1, the first version of which was announced in April, can engage in multistep reasoning and handle complex processes, manage digital workflows, and deploy new features in enterprise systems, the company says. Meta is a bit behind its competitors here; Anthropic and OpenAI have offered similar models for quite some time. “Muse Spark 1.1 delivers exceptional performance in personal agentic tasks that require planning and orchestration across a range of external apps and services,” the company wrote in a blog post. The Muse Spark release was apparently important enough to compel CEO Mark Zuckerberg to post on X for the first time in three years. Other releases this week have included a new version of Grok from SpaceXAI and a new family of models from OpenAI, GPT-5.6, that also dropped Thursday.","score":0.8342047,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://ai.meta.com/blog/introducing-muse-spark-meta-model-api","title":"Introducing Muse Spark 1.1","content":"# Introducing Muse Spark 1.1. Today, we’re excited to introduce Muse Spark 1.1, the latest model from Meta Superintelligence Labs and a significant upgrade from Muse Spark. Muse Spark 1.1 is a multimodal reasoning model built for agentic tasks, with major gains in tool and computer use, coding, and multimodal understanding. With these improvements, Muse Spark 1.1 advances the performance-efficiency frontier. Along with this release, we are launching a public preview of the new Meta Model API where developers can access Muse Spark 1.1. The model is available now in "Thinking" mode in the Meta AI app and on meta.ai. Muse Spark 1.1 delivers exceptional performance in personal agentic tasks that require planning and orchestration across a range of external apps and services. Along with coding and agentic capabilities, Muse Spark 1.1 excels in perception, multimodal reasoning, and tool use. For the first time, developers can begin building with Muse Spark 1.1 via the new Meta Model API, now in public preview.","score":0.8259413,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yQpFUVqYKFA","title":"Meta's Biggest AI Launch Yet | Muse Spark 1.1 Explained","content":"Meta's Biggest AI Launch Yet | Muse Spark 1.1 Explained

🚀 Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 Explained | The AI Agent That Could Challenge GPT, Claude & Gemini

Meta has just unveiled one of its biggest AI updates ever, introducing Muse Spark 1.1, alongside Muse Image, Muse Video, and the new Meta Model API.

Muse Spark 1.1 isn't just another chatbot. It's a next-generation multimodal AI agent designed to reason, write code, use computers, orchestrate multiple agents, and complete complex tasks with minimal human guidance.

Could this be Meta's biggest step toward personal superintelligence?

In this video, we break down everything you need to know about Muse Spark 1.1, how it works, what's new compared to the original Muse Spark, and why the AI community is paying close attention.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

📌 In this video

• What is Muse Spark 1.1?
• What's new compared to Muse Spark?
• Multi-agent reasoning explained
• Computer use and agentic workflows
• Major coding improvements
• Muse Image & Muse Video explained
• Meta Model API for developers
• Benchmarks vs GPT, Claude, Gemini & other frontier models
• Meta's vision for personal superintelligence

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
